_note: Dialogue templates shaped by disposition tier and personality axes. {name}, {title}, {pronoun.subject}, {pronoun.object}, {pronoun.possessive}, {pronoun.subject_cap}, {ship_name}, {system}, {faction} are valid placeholders.

hostile: {name} sees you and {pronoun.possessive} expression hardens. "You have nothing I want. Leave."
{name} turns away before you can speak. The message is clear.
"I know who you are," {name} says flatly. "We're done."
cold: A curt nod from {name}. Nothing more.
{name} acknowledges you with the minimum courtesy {pronoun.possessive} position requires.
neutral: {name} looks you over. "Captain. What can I do for you?"
"You're the one with the {ship_name}," {name} says. "What do you need?"
{name} sets down what {pronoun.subject}'s working on. "Go ahead."
warm: {name} waves you over. "Good timing. Sit down."
"Captain! I was hoping you'd come by," {name} says, making room at the table.
{name} greets you by name. There's genuine warmth in it.
friendly: {name} breaks into a real smile when {pronoun.subject} sees you. "It's good to see you again."
"There {pronoun.subject} is," {name} says, pushing a drink across the counter. "I've been saving this."
{name} clasps your hand. The kind of greeting you earn, not receive.
trusted: {name} pulls you aside, away from listening ears. "I'm glad you're here. We need to talk."
"I trust you," {name} says simply. "Not many people I say that to."
{name} looks relieved. "Thank the stars. I need someone I can be honest with."

positive: "Last time you were here, {summary}. I haven't forgotten."
"I remember — {summary}. People like you are rare out here."
negative: "You're the one who — {summary}." A pause. "I remember."
{name}'s jaw tightens. "After {summary}, you've got nerve coming back."
neutral_memory: "You've been through here before. {summary}, wasn't it?"

warmth_high: {name} takes a breath — {pronoun.subject}'s been waiting to talk to someone who'd listen.
"Since you're asking —" {name} settles in, clearly prepared to talk.
warmth_low: A pause. {name} decides you're worth a few words.
boldness_high: "Here's what I know, and I'm not sugarcoating it.
boldness_low: "I've heard some things, but don't quote me on this.
"This is just what I've picked up — I can't confirm all of it.

faction_intel: "You didn't hear this from me, but {knowledge_item}."
thread_rumor: "There's been talk of something. {knowledge_item}. I'd look into it."
"Word is — {knowledge_item}. Make of that what you will."
"I keep hearing about — {knowledge_item}. Might be worth your time."
connection_mention: "You should talk to {connection_name} at {connection_location}. {pronoun.subject_cap} knows more than I do."
"If you want the real story, find {connection_name}. Tell {connection_object} I sent you."

colleague: "{connection_name}? We work together. {context}. Solid enough, as far as that goes."
rival: A tight smile. "{connection_name}. {context}. We don't see eye to eye."
dependent: "We need each other. {context}. That's just how it works out here."
old_friend: "We go back. {context}. One of the few I'd trust with my life."
knows_of: "I know the name. {context}. Never dealt with {connection_object} directly."

cold: "I don't do business with people I don't know. Or trust."
hostile: "You think I'd give you work? After what you've done?"
{name} doesn't even dignify it with a response.
"Get out of my office before I call security."
The conversation is over before it started.
cold: {name} goes back to work without looking up.
neutral: {name} nods. "You know where to find me."
warm: "Come back anytime. And be careful out there."
friendly: "Take care of yourself. I mean it."
"Next time you're through, first drink is on me."
trusted: "Stay alive. The galaxy needs more people like you."
"If you need anything — anything — you know how to reach me."
